query: Can you recon issue #412 and give me a level-of-effort estimate before we dispatch?
note: 'Recon' + LoE on a groomed issue before dispatch = textbook technical-recon.

query: Size this ticket for me — trace it into the codebase and tell me how it'd actually be built.
note: Size + trace into code + the HOW = dev-side technical recon.

query: These five stories are groomed and accepted. Tech-review each one and tell me which are safe to build.
note: Batch tech-review of already-groomed issues for buildability = technical-recon.

query: Before I send an agent at JIRA-220, I need to know how big it is and what it might break.
note: LoE + risk assessment per-issue pre-dispatch = technical-recon.

query: Estimate the effort on #88 and recommend whether it should be one PR or split.
note: Effort estimate + split recommendation is a core technical-recon deliverable.

query: Is this groomed issue actually ready for a dev to pick up, or does it need more work first?
note: Deciding a groomed issue is dev-vetted/dispatch-ready = technical-recon's verdict.

query: Assess the implementation approach for the new export feature ticket and pin the model and effort for dispatch.
note: Implementation approach + pin dispatch model/effort = technical-recon sections 1 and 7.

query: Scope #501 — figure out which modules change, the risks, and what it depends on.
note: Scope + affected modules + risks + dependencies = the recon findings comment.

query: We accepted this story with the stakeholders but nobody on the dev team has vetted how to build it. Can you do that pass?
note: Stakeholder-accepted but dev-unvetted is the exact gap technical-recon closes.

query: This ticket is too vague — I can't even tell what the stakeholder wants. Clarify the intent first.
note: 'Vague intent / what do they want' is pre-dev grooming, upstream of recon.

query: Groom these raw issues and get the story right before any tech review.
note: 'Groom' + explicitly before tech review = grooming-issues, not recon.

query: Spelunk the whole codebase and figure out the conventions we use everywhere.
note: 'Spelunk' + whole-codebase conventions = spelunking-specs, not per-issue recon.

query: Recon the codebase and spec out how the payments module is wired.
note: 'Recon' here means map the app into specs, not size an issue.

query: Triage this stub bug report and decide if it's ready-to-dispatch or needs more spec input.
note: Cheap intake-triage of a stub is the issue-filing recon, explicitly NOT technical-recon.

query: Set the loe:M label and ready-to-dispatch on #77.
note: Just applying control-plane labels, not performing the recon that earns them.

query: Dispatch a coding agent to implement #310 in a worktree and open a PR.
note: Actual implementation dispatch, downstream of recon's read-only pass.

query: Fill your slots and keep eight agents working the queue.
note: Orchestration loop, not a per-issue sizing pass.

query: Convert the messy CSV in my downloads into a clean spreadsheet with totals.
note: Truly unrelated — spreadsheet work, nothing to do with issue recon.

query: File a follow-up issue for the tech debt I just noticed while working.
note: Creating a new follow-up issue, not reconning an existing groomed one.
